operating_state: Total revenue increased 18% to $195.9 billion driven by 16% comparable sales growth and 22 new warehouse openings, reaching 815 locations globally with 61.7 million paid members. E-commerce achieved 44% comparable sales growth representing 7% of total sales, while membership fees increased 9% with renewal rates maintaining 91% in core markets and Executive membership penetration reaching 55%.
cost_structure: Gross margin percentage decreased seven basis points to 11.13% primarily due to gasoline price inflation, though core merchandise categories improved 23 basis points. SG&A expenses as percentage of net sales decreased 40 basis points to 9.61% driven by payroll leveraging from increased sales and the end of COVID-19 incremental wages in February 2021. The company permanently increased warehouse employee wages effective March 2021.
material_events: The company paid a special dividend of $10.00 per share totaling $4.4 billion and increased quarterly dividend from $0.70 to $0.79 per share. Share repurchases totaled $495 million for 1.4 million shares under the $4.0 billion authorization program with $3.25 billion remaining available through April 2023. Joseph Portera announced retirement as Executive Vice President with Yoram Rubananko promoted to Executive Vice President, Northeast and Southeast Regions.
macro_exposures: COVID-related supply and logistics constraints adversely affected merchandise categories and are expected to continue for the foreseeable future. Gasoline prices increased 12% positively impacting net sales by 100 basis points, while certain merchandise categories experienced inflation higher than recent years. Foreign currency changes positively impacted net sales by 169 basis points, primarily from Canadian and Other International operations.
forward_outlook: Management plans to open approximately 35 new warehouses including five relocations in fiscal 2022 and expects capital expenditures of $3.8 to $4.2 billion. The company anticipates higher rates of operating floor space square footage growth in foreign markets due to smaller base. Capital allocation priorities include ongoing investments in warehouse expansion, supply chain infrastructure, and maintaining competitive employee compensation.
uncertainty_profile: The company faces ongoing legal proceedings including California labor law class actions regarding wage and hour practices with potential civil penalties. Ongoing tax examinations across multiple jurisdictions including a current California audit may result in material adjustments. Real estate and construction delays from COVID-19 create uncertainty around warehouse opening timelines and capital expenditure execution, while self-insurance liabilities could fluctuate due to inflation rates.
